out_of_scope_note: Methods/software release paper introducing DREDge, a registration algorithm for motion correction in high-density extracellular electrophysiology recordings. The paper is a preprocessing/infrastructure tool for spike sorting and probe-motion tracking — analogous to fMRIPrep-class pipeline releases the prompt lists as out-of-scope. It engages no theoretical framework of perception, decision-making, encoding, or cognition; contributions are algorithmic (AP/LFP-band motion estimation, registration robustness) and benchmark-style (reduced spike-sorting error, recovered evoked potentials). Useful downstream infrastructure for neural recording labs, but lacks the computational-neuroscience theoretical claim that would let it match researcher project lenses.
